subject: Re: errors during ts_etomo_patches and ts_reconstruct
body:
Dear Daniel,

Thank you for your comments! Some people might find your comments helpful. 
Yes, Warp was working fine with my old OS, at least with dev32.
I started encountering issues with dev34. 
Finally, to keep up with WarpTools' future improvements, I updated my OS. 
Thanks again!!!
Reika 

> On Jul 24, 2025, at 3:36 PM, Daniel Cai <dxhcai@gmail.com> wrote:
> 
> Hi Reika,
> 
> I got this error and downgraded to dev32 and it was fine. Not sure what version you’re on thougg.
> 
> Daniel
> 
> Get Outlook for iOS <https://aka.ms/o0ukef>
> From: warp-em@googlegroups.com <warp-em@googlegroups.com> on behalf of Reika Watanabe <reika.watanabe.c@gmail.com>
> Sent: Thursday, July 24, 2025 3:32:48 PM
> To: Warp <warp-em@googlegroups.com>
> Subject: Re: errors during ts_etomo_patches and ts_reconstruct
>  
> Dear Alister,
> 
> Thanks for your reply...! I understand. However, Warp_tools worked before with this Linux...
> That does not make sense to me....
> Should I reinstall the Warp from the beginning?
> 
> Reika 
> 
> 
> 
> On Thursday, July 24, 2025 at 3:10:52 PM UTC-7 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
> Hi Reika,
> 
> glibc 2.17 was released in 2012, almost 15 years ago… sorry but we cannot support platforms like this, you would have to find your own solution in this case
> 
> Cheers,
> 
> Alister
> 
> 
> Sent from mobile - apologies for brevity
> 
>> On Jul 24, 2025, at 14:43, Reika Watanabe <reika.wa...@gmail.com <>> wrote:
>> 
>> ﻿
> 
>> Dear Alister and others,
>> 
>> I am using the newest version of warp with CentOS 7.9.
>> 
>> # Name                    Version                   Build  Channel
>> warp                      2.0.0dev34              py311_0    warpem
>> 
>> LSB Version: :core-4.1-amd64:core-4.1-ia32:core-4.1-noarch
>> Distributor ID: CentOS
>> Description: CentOS Linux release 7.9.2009 (Core)
>> Release: 7.9.2009
>> Codename: Core
>> 
>> I cannot run ts_etomo_patches. (error message attached below)
>> Since I could not run ts_etomo_patches, I did ts_stack. I  got the error message (error message below) but at least I can find the tiltstack folder created and I can find aligned tilt. After manual alignment with Etomo, I imported alignment,  and  I tried to run ts_reconstruct. I again got the error message and reconstruction folder is empty.
>> After investigating error message with ChatGPT, it seems that my glibc version is too old. 2.17.
>> What do you recommend ?
>> Are there any way to avoid making png?
>> 
>> WarpTools used to work well without error with this linux machine and I am using the same data set it used to work.
>> Thank you very much for your kind attention in advance.
>>  With best wishes,
>> Reika 
>> 
>> 
>> ts_etomo_patches error messages
>> 
>> No alternative input specified, will use input parameters from warp_tiltseries.setting
>> File search will be relative to /mnt/staging/rwatanabe/DataProcess/Warp_tool/old_data_MARVdel_test/tomostar
>> 1 files found
>> Parsing previous results for each item, if available...

>> Check logs in /mnt/staging/rwatanabe/DataProcess/Warp_tool/old_data_MARVdel_test/warp_tiltseries/logs for more info.
>> Use the change_selection WarpTool to reactivate this item if required.
